name: NF-kB inhibition improved aged muscle repair in mice
summary: Inhibiting age-associated NF-kB signaling in myofibers or downstream inflammatory lipid signaling improved aged mouse muscle repair.
statement: Age-associated NF-kB signaling in differentiated myofibers impaired nearby satellite-cell function through cell-non-autonomous mechanisms, and NF-kB or downstream pathway inhibition improved repair in aged mouse muscle.
population_or_model: Aged mouse skeletal-muscle injury and satellite-cell niche assays
time_horizon: Aged muscle repair after genetic and pharmacological NF-kB-pathway modulation
quantitative_note: The staged baseline treats sodium salicylate context as animal-level intervention support, not human proof.
caveats: NF-kB modulation is broad and could affect immune defense, inflammation, and repair differently by tissue and dose.
Aged muscle repair after injury is not equivalent to durable human functional rejuvenation.
